chapters.title: Measurement
key_facts: SI unit of length is metre (m)
SI unit of mass is kilogram (kg)
misconceptions: Weight and mass are the same — FALSE
chapters.hero_hook: How do scientists measure the width of a single hair?

chapters.title: Motion
key_facts: Distance is scalar; displacement is vector
misconceptions: A fast object is always accelerating — FALSE
chapters.hero_hook: Why does a spinning top not fall?

chapters.title: Force and Pressure
key_facts: Buoyant force equals weight of fluid displaced — Archimedes' Principle
chapters.hero_hook: Why can you walk on snow with snowshoes but sink without them?

chapters.title: Light
key_facts: Light travels at 3×10⁸ m/s in vacuum
Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection
White light splits into 7 colours via prism
Refraction causes bending of light at interface
chapters.hero_hook: Why does a straw appear bent in water?

chapters.title: Heat
key_facts: Water has unusually high specific heat: 4200 J/kg°C
Heat flows from hot to cold
chapters.hero_hook: Why does a metal spoon feel colder than a wooden spoon at the same temperature?

chapters.title: Sound
key_facts: Sound travels at ~340 m/s in air
Frequency range of human hearing: 20 Hz–20,000 Hz
Echo = sound reflected off a surface
chapters.hero_hook: How do bats navigate in total darkness?

chapters.title: Electricity
chapters.hero_hook: How does a tiny wire glow to light up a room?

chapters.title: Laws of Motion
key_facts: First Law: Objects at rest stay at rest unless acted upon
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction
chapters.hero_hook: Why do you jerk forward when a bus brakes suddenly?

chapters.title: Work, Energy and Power
key_facts: Energy cannot be created or destroyed
chapters.hero_hook: A roller coaster: where does the energy go?

chapters.title: Electromagnetism
key_facts: Current produces a magnetic field (Oersted's discovery)
Lenz's Law: induced current opposes change
chapters.hero_hook: How does a power plant 200 km away light your house?

chapters.title: Motion in One Dimension
chapters.hero_hook: Galileo drops two balls from the Tower of Pisa — what happens?

chapters.title: Gravitation
chapters.hero_hook: What keeps the Moon from falling — and why does it orbit instead?

chapters.title: Wave Motion and Sound
key_facts: Doppler Effect: frequency shifts with relative motion
chapters.hero_hook: Why does a siren pitch change as an ambulance passes?

chapters.title: Electricity and Circuits
key_facts: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law: ΣV = 0 in a loop
chapters.hero_hook: How does a smart fuse know exactly when to blow?

chapters.title: Light — Optics
chapters.hero_hook: How fibre optics carry the entire internet at the speed of light

chapters.title: Radioactivity and Nuclear Physics
key_facts: Half-life is the time for half the atoms to decay
chapters.hero_hook: The equation E=mc² that ended a war and powers the stars
